title: CR-013 B7: pipeline — enforce dedupe-before-window ordering
description:
From CR-013 Story 4 (interaction).

Scope:
- Reorder the query pipeline so dedupe runs BEFORE window construction. Window work (B3) is invoked only on the surviving kept hits.
- A hit that dedupe drops must NEVER have a window constructed for it.
- Two surviving hits that are far enough apart to survive dedupe but whose windows overlap: render each window independently. No stitching, no de-overlap.

File(s):
- query orchestrator / pipeline entrypoint

Acceptance (pipeline-order test):
- Construct a scenario where window-build is instrumented; assert it is called only for surviving hits.
- Assert that reversing the order (window-then-dedupe) would fail this test (guard against regression).
- Overlapping-windows scenario: both hits render full windows independently.

Depends on: B3 (sv-lu7), B6 (sv-xlf).
Blocks: (none).

title: cli/eval_cmd.py — scripvec eval run
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/eval_cmd.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADR-007. Flags `--queries`, `--judgments`, `--index` default latest, `--format`. Calls `eval.run`. JSON output with metrics table, per-bucket stratified table, ship pass/fail flags, failures_path. `--format text` renders ASCII.

Error map: harness RuntimeError → USER_ERROR `eval_failed`; sanity-probe fail → USER_ERROR `sanity_probe_failed`.

Blocks on: errors, eval.run.

title: cr-014: cli/test_exclude_contracts.py — dense happy path, empty results, JSON shape
description:
CR-014 B9. Contract tests for scripvec query --exclude under --mode dense (the default).

Cases:
- Happy path: --exclude returns results drawn from outside the exclusion set; response JSON contains exclude block with text, set_size, excluded_verse_ids.
- Empty-after-exclusion: construct an exclude text that (against the fixture corpus) covers every hit in the main query's k + exclude_buffer window. Expect results: [] and a fully populated exclude block. Exit code 0, not an error. Covers Story 6.
- Response shape: JSON includes exclude block only when --exclude is supplied; key is absent (not null) otherwise.

Blocks on: B4, B5.

title: eval/run.py — orchestrator + ship criteria
description:
File: `packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/run.py`.

Per CR-002 + CR-001. Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ShipCriteria` — `hybrid_beats_bm25_recall10`, `dense_beats_bm25_recall10`, `index_size_under_400mb`. Module constants: `HYBRID_VS_BM25_PCT=5`, `DENSE_VS_BM25_PCT=5`, `MAX_INDEX_BYTES=400*1024*1024`.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ModeMetrics` — mode, recall_at_10, recall_at_20, ndcg_at_10, mrr_at_10, latency_p50_ms, latency_p95_ms.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class EvalReport` — index_hash, metrics (tuple of ModeMetrics for bm25/dense/hybrid), recall10_by_bucket (mode → bucket → float), ship, failures_path.
- `run(queries_path, judgments_path, *, index="latest") -> EvalReport` — load + validate, run sanity probes, run every query × every mode, capture latency, compute metrics + stratification, evaluate ship criteria, write `failures_<timestamp>.jsonl`, return EvalReport.
- Private `_compute_ship`, `_write_failures`, `_index_dir_size_bytes`, `FailureRow`.

All failures propagate (ADR-001). Blocks on: metrics, dataset, retrieval.query/bm25/store/paths/manifest.

title: retrieval/scope.py — Scope dataclass + canonical normalization
description:
Define the Scope dataclass (volume|None, book|None, range|None). Canonicalize each field using the ADR-010 book/volume catalog and the ADR-010/011/012 reference parser.

Acceptance:
- Scope.from_flags(volume=..., book=..., range=...) returns a canonicalized Scope or raises a typed error.
- Unknown volume / unknown book / malformed range raise typed errors (messages handled in F2 bead).
- Unit tests cover canonicalization of each field independently.

Source: CR-011 (docs/specs/change_requests/staged/cr-011_cli_scripture_scope_filters.md) — Beads plan foundation F1.

title: CR-013 B4: retrieval/dedupe.py — proximity-dedupe walk + config M
description:
From CR-013 Story 2 (dedupe machinery).

Scope:
- New module: packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/dedupe.py
- Function: given a list of candidate hits (pre-sorted by score desc), an integer M (proximity cutoff in verses), and an integer k (target top-K), return (kept_hits, dropped_count).
- Algorithm: walk desc-by-score; a hit is kept iff no already-kept hit is within M verses of it AND shares its chapter/section/book. Truncate kept to k.
- Cross-chapter / cross-book: two hits in different chapters or books are NEVER considered within M of each other (regardless of verse-number arithmetic).
- M and k_buffer (the multiplier on k for how many candidates to pull) live in the project-root config — do not hardcode.

Acceptance (unit tests):
- Same-chapter close pair → lower-scored dropped.
- Cross-chapter close verse-numbers → both kept.
- Score ordering respected (higher score wins the keep).
- dropped count is correct under various inputs.
- Kept list truncated to exactly k (or fewer, if fewer kept).

Depends on: (none — pure function).
Blocks: B6.

title: retrieval/config.py — EmbedConfig + env loader
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/config.py`.

Per ADR-005 + CR-001 § Function plan. Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class EmbedConfig` — `base_url, api_key, model, dim` with non-empty invariants.
- `load_embed_config() -> EmbedConfig` — env first (`OPENAI_BASE_URL`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, model id, dim), optional non-committed config file second. Never hardcode. Raises `RuntimeError` if any field cannot be resolved (ADR-001).
- Private `_read_optional_config_file() -> dict` — returns empty dict if absent; raises only on corrupt file.

Accept: unit tests for missing env (raises), valid env (returns config), corrupt optional file (raises).

title: cr-014: cli/query.py — reject --exclude with --mode bm25 as user error
description:
CR-014 B6. When the user passes both --exclude and --mode bm25, raise a user-facing error and exit with the CLI's existing user-error exit code. Do not execute the query; do not make any embed call.

Error message must name both the flag (--exclude) and the mode (--mode bm25) and state that vector exclusion has no BM25 analog. A lexical-negation variant is a separate CR.

ADR-001: silent no-op would violate loud-failure posture.

Acceptance:
- Running scripvec query '<main>' --exclude '<avoid>' --mode bm25 exits non-zero with the message above and no embed call or vector search.

No dependencies on this CR's other beads. Blocks: B10.

title: retrieval/query.py — retrieval buffer widening for scoped queries
description:
Before applying scope, retrieve more than K candidates so the top-K survives typical scope drops. Buffer size is a configurable value living in the project-root config file (ADR-policy: ADRs do not stipulate numeric values for tunable parameters).

Acceptance:
- A scoped query whose scope keeps >= K hits returns exactly K.
- A scoped query whose scope keeps < K hits returns all that survive (possibly zero); no padding from out-of-scope.
- Buffer size is read from config, not hard-coded.
- Unscoped query path is byte-identical to today.

Source: CR-011 Beads plan foundation F4.

title: CR-015 BEAD F: Attach cross-reference companions to each top-K hit
description:
For indices carrying cross-reference metadata, when `--cross-ref-expand N > 0`, append `cross_references` to each result in `results[]`. Companions ordered by the corpus's own footnote ordering, capped at `N`, shape `{ref, text, tag}`, nested under their parent (never injected into top-K). Dedup: companions that are themselves in the primary `results[]` are suppressed from that hit's companion list. Pin the empty-companions convention (`[]` vs. absent) by contract test.

Acceptance criteria:
- Fixture index with known cross-references produces exactly the expected `cross_references` arrays at `N=3` (contract test).
- `rank` fields on parent results are unaffected by companion expansion (regression test).
- Dedup covered by a fixture where a verse appears both as a top-K hit and as another hit's cross-reference; the companion entry is suppressed.
- Empty-companions shape is consistent with the chosen convention (`[]` everywhere OR field absent on zero-companion hits), pinned by test.

Source: CR-015, Story 6.

title: retrieval/query.py — ADR-014 extracted-reference bounding by scope
description:
Where ADR-014 force-inclusion runs, intersect extracted references with the Scope from F1 before forcing. Log dropped references with their scope-conflict reason.

Acceptance (Story F):
- Force-inclusion per ADR-014 still applies for extracted references that fall inside the scope.
- Extracted references outside the scope are dropped, not silently injected.
- A log entry records the dropped reference and the scope that excluded it.
- Unscoped queries preserve ADR-014 behavior byte-identically.

Source: CR-011 Story F (bead F-story).

title: retrieval/paths.py — data dir helpers + latest symlink
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/paths.py`.

Implement data-dir layout helpers per CR-001. All other modules ask this one, nobody else joins strings:
- `data_dir()` — honors `SCRIPVEC_DATA_DIR`, default `./data/`.
- `raw_dir()`, `eval_dir()`, `logs_dir()`, `indexes_dir()`, `index_path(hash)`.
- `latest_symlink()`, `resolve_latest() -> str` (raises if missing), `set_latest(hash)` (atomic swap via `os.replace`).
- `index_path` validates hash against `^[0-9a-f]{32}$` regex.

No in-repo imports. No silent fallback to mtime (ADR-001).

title: cr-014: retrieval/test_embed_serialization.py — embed calls never overlap with --exclude
description:
CR-014 B13. Contract test enforcing ADR-006 serialization when --exclude is used.

Approach:
- Instrument or mock the embed client so each call records a (start_ts, end_ts) pair.
- Run scripvec query with --exclude under --mode dense; assert no two embed intervals overlap.
- Run scripvec query with --exclude under --mode hybrid; assert no two embed intervals overlap. BM25 work MAY overlap with embeds; embed-with-embed MUST NOT.
- Order of the two embed calls is not fixed by the test — assert non-overlap only.

This bead explicitly tests the edge that the CR calls out: two embed calls on one query must still run serially.

Blocks on: B4, B8.

title: cr-014: retrieval/exclude.py — compute_exclusion_set(text, M) embeds and vector-searches
description:
CR-014 B2. Implement compute_exclusion_set(text: str, m: int) -> list[str] in a new module packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/exclude.py.

Behavior:
- Embeds text via the ADR-005 embed client (same model, endpoint, 8K-token cap as the main query dense path).
- Runs a vector search against the index for the top-m most-similar verses.
- Returns the verse_ids as a list.
- Raises (ADR-001) on any upstream error — no silent fallback.

ADR-006: this is one of two embed calls per --exclude query; the caller (CLI layer) is responsible for serializing. This module makes one sequential call and returns.

Acceptance:
- Unit test with a stubbed embed client and in-memory index returns the expected verse_ids.
- Upstream error from embed client propagates.

Blocks on: B1 (config). Blocks: B4, B8.

title: retrieval/manifest.py + test — index manifest + hash
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/manifest.py` and `test_manifest.py`.

Per CR-002 + CR-001. Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class Manifest` with fields per CR-001 § Function plan: `corpus_source, corpus_commit_sha, tokenizer_version, embed_endpoint, embed_model, embed_dim, embed_normalized, bm25_lib, bm25_lib_version, bm25_k1, bm25_b, vec_schema_version`. ADR-009 chunker params (per-chunk cap, no-split floor) must also be included in the hashed manifest per that ADR; add `nonverse_chunk_cap` and `nonverse_chunk_floor` fields.
- `config_hash(m) -> str` — BLAKE2b-128 over canonical JSON (delegates to hashing.py).
- `write_manifest(m, path)` — atomic write-temp-then-replace.
- `read_manifest(path) -> Manifest` — strict. Missing or extra fields raise (ADR-001).

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded: round-trip, missing required → raise, unknown field → raise, type mismatch → raise.

Blocks on: hashing.py.

title: cli/query_cmd.py — wire --range flag
description:
Thread --range through the scripvec query command, accepting the full ADR-010 / ADR-011 / ADR-012 reference-list grammar. Reuse F1/F2/F3/F4.

Accepted examples (all must parse):
- 'Alma 30-42'
- 'D&C 76'
- '2 Nephi 31:1-21'
- 'Alma 32; 34:15-41'

Acceptance (Story C):
- --range hits are filtered to the reference set, verse-inclusive where verses are specified and chapter-inclusive where not (ADR-011).
- Malformed range raises a specific parse error per ADR-010 grammar (not a generic 'invalid input').
- --range that contradicts supplied --volume or --book raises per ADR-001 (e.g., --book alma --range 'Helaman 5').
- Contract test covers the flag.

Source: CR-011 Story C (bead C1).

title: reference/reference.py — full grammar (single, range, list, extraction)
description:
File: `packages/reference/src/scripvec_reference/reference.py`.

Implements ADRs 010, 011, 012, 013 in one file (one-agent ownership avoids same-file contention). Required public surface:

- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class Reference` — book/chapter/verse with the invariants from CR-001 § Function plan.
- `parse_reference(s) -> Reference` — ADR-010 strict single-citation grammar. No abbreviations, case variants, leading zeros, alt punctuation.
- `parse_range(s) -> tuple[Reference, Reference]` — ADR-011 `<ref> - <ref>` (ASCII hyphen, surrounding spaces). Reversed-order raises.
- `parse_list(s) -> list[Reference|Range]` — ADR-012 `;`-separated; dedupe valid duplicates; malformed item names its ordinal in the raise.
- `extract_references(text) -> list[...]` — ADR-013 free-text scan using the single-ref + range grammars (not list). No-candidate case returns empty (not a raise).
- `canonical(book, chapter, verse) -> str` — round-trips with parse_reference.
- Private `_split_book_and_loc` per CR-001.

Blocks on: books.py. Accept: unit tests per ADRs 010-013 (positive + explicit negative cases).

title: eval/dataset.py + test — JSONL loaders + sanity probes
description:
Files: `packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/dataset.py` and `test_dataset.py`.

Per CR-002 + CR-001. Implement:
- `QueryRow`, `Judgment`, `SanityProbe` dataclasses with invariants from CR-001 § Function plan.
- `SANITY_PROBES: tuple[SanityProbe, ...]` — module constant. Placeholder values (filled in authoring bead); constant surface must exist now.
- `load_queries(path) -> list[QueryRow]` — JSONL parse; raises on duplicate query text/id, malformed row, <8 per canonical bucket (doctrinal/narrative/phrase-memory/proper-noun).
- `load_judgments(path, known_verse_ids) -> list[Judgment]` — raises on unknown verse_id, grade ∉ {1,2}, malformed row.
- `run_sanity_probes(idx: Bm25Index)` — raises if any probe's expected verses don't appear in BM25 top-3.
- Private `_parse_query_row`, `_parse_judgment_row` (line numbers in raise messages).

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded: well-formed accept, duplicate text/id raises, malformed JSON raises (line number), under-8-per-bucket raises, unknown verse_id raises, invalid grade (0 or 3) raises.

Blocks on: bm25.py (for sanity probe runtime).

title: corpus_ingest/verse.py — VerseRecord + verse_id slug
description:
File: `packages/corpus_ingest/src/scripvec_corpus_ingest/verse.py`.

Per CR-001 § File blueprint, design question 1 (resolved 2026-04-20: slug). Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class VerseRecord` — fields `verse_id, ref_canonical, book, chapter, verse, text` with invariants from CR-001.
- `make_verse_id(ref_canonical) -> str` — stable human-readable slug (`1-nephi-3-7`, `dandc-88-118`). Lowercase, replace ` `/`:` with `-`, `&` with `and`. Raises on empty input.

No in-repo imports. Accept: unit test round-trips a few canonical refs.

title: cli/test_contracts.py — scope error-message contract tests (ADR-001)
description:
For each Story G failure case, assert non-zero exit and the required message substring per ADR-001. Test-only bead; the messages themselves are produced by F2.

Acceptance (Story G):
- --book against D&C: exit non-zero, stderr contains the exact string 'D&C has sections, not books; use --range instead'.
- --book whose canonical volume != supplied --volume: exit non-zero, message names both the book and the conflicting volume.
- --range whose reference is outside supplied --volume or --book: exit non-zero, message names the conflicting filter and the reference.
- Unknown volume: exit non-zero, distinct message.
- Unknown book: exit non-zero, distinct message.
- Malformed range: exit non-zero, ADR-010 grammar parse-error message.
- No scope-consistency failure is silent: all tests assert non-zero exit.

Source: CR-011 Story G (bead G1).

title: cr-014: retrieval/exclude.py — filter_by_exclusion(hits, exclusion_set)
description:
CR-014 B3. Implement filter_by_exclusion(hits: list, exclusion_set: set[str]) -> list in the same packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/exclude.py module as B2.

Behavior:
- Drops any hit whose verse_id is in exclusion_set.
- Preserves ranking order of surviving hits.
- Pure function, no side effects, no IO. Safe to call from either the dense path or the hybrid pre-RRF filter.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests: empty exclusion_set returns hits unchanged; exclusion covers all hits returns empty list; partial overlap returns the correct subset in original order.

No dependencies. Blocks: B4, B8.

title: corpus_ingest/chunker.py — ADR-009 non-verse sentence-packer
description:
File: `packages/corpus_ingest/src/scripvec_corpus_ingest/chunker.py`.

Per ADR-009. Handles non-verse canonical text from bcbooks: BoM title page, Testimony of Three/Eight Witnesses, pre-1921 book-level headings, the 15 in-text 1830 chapter headings (Mosiah 9/23; Alma 5/7/9/17/21/36/38/39/45; Helaman 7/13; 3 Nephi 11; Mormon), D&C `signature` field.

Policy (ADR-009):
- Chunk = one or more consecutive sentences.
- Greedy pack to the per-chunk token cap.
- No-split floor: items with token count ≤ floor emitted whole regardless of cap.
- Invariant `floor > cap` asserted at config load — raise if violated.
- **Token counter must be the same counter as `embed.py`'s 8K gate** (shared via embed module's public surface).
- Sentence splitter library choice is local (NLTK punkt / regex — must be deterministic and offline).
- Emit chunk records with same retrieval shape as `VerseRecord` plus metadata (`kind`, source item id, position within source) so retrieval can't tell them apart during RRF.

Chunker params (`nonverse_chunk_cap`, `nonverse_chunk_floor`) read from project-root config file (not stipulated in ADR; file introduced here or follow-on CR). For MVP, accept a transitional code-local placeholder per ADR-009 § Conflicts; document as placeholder.

Blocks on: embed.py (token counter).

title: cli/version_cmd.py — scripvec --version
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/version_cmd.py`.

Per ADR-007 + CR-002. JSON output `{cli_version, embedding_model, latest_index_hash}`. Implement `register(app)` wiring `version` callback + `--version` flag. On missing config or missing latest, routes through `errors.emit_error` with stable code.

Blocks on: config.py, paths.py, errors.py.

title: BM25-mode relative floor culls results below ratio of top score
description:
Slice: applies --floor under --mode bm25.

Depends on: sv-lo9 (flag surface). Independent of dense and hybrid semantic beads.

Acceptance criteria:
- Under --mode bm25, hits with score < floor.value * top_hit_bm25_score are dropped before top-K is returned.
- When the underlying query returns zero hits (no top hit exists), effective_threshold is 0.0 and 'results' is empty; no divide-by-zero or other error.
- --floor 1.0 keeps only hits tied with the top score.
- --floor 0.0 is a no-op.
- Unit test: fixture query with top hit 24.7, --floor 0.3 drops all hits below 7.41.
- Unit test: zero-hit query with --floor 0.5 returns empty results cleanly.

Source: docs/specs/change_requests/staged/cr-012_cli_mode_aware_similarity_floor.md (Bead B3).

title: CR-013 B9: eval — report recall@k with and without --dedupe
description:
From CR-013 'Impact on referenced docs → Principle 001'.

Scope:
- Update scripvec eval run (packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/run.py) so the harness records recall@k for the same query set under BOTH conditions:
  (a) --dedupe ON (the default)
  (b) --dedupe OFF (raw retrieval ordering)
- Both numbers land in the eval report so dedupe's effect on ranked metrics is visible per Principle 001.
- Window (--window) is NOT toggled by this bead. Window is presentation-layer and orthogonal to retrieval recall; eval stays window-off.

File(s):
- packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/run.py
- eval metrics wiring if needed

Acceptance:
- Existing eval tests still pass.
- New eval output carries recall@k_dedupe_on and recall@k_dedupe_off for every query set it runs against.

Depends on: B4 (sv-1v1), B6 (sv-xlf).
Blocks: (none).

title: cli/test_contracts.py — ADR-007 per-subcommand contract tests
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/test_contracts.py`.

ADR-007 validation clause. One success-path and one forced-failure test per subcommand (`query`, `index build`, `index list`, `eval run`, `feedback`, `--version`). Uses Typer's CliRunner; `json.loads` on stdout/stderr.

Specific tests per CR-001 § File blueprint test listings:
- query: documented JSON schema on success; structured stderr on bad flag.
- index build: schema (index_hash, latest); drift → structured stderr.
- index list: array shape with hash/created_at/model/dim/is_latest; unreadable dir → structured stderr.
- eval run: schema (metrics, recall10_by_bucket, ship, failures_path, index_hash); unknown verse_id in judgments → structured stderr code `unknown_verse_id`.
- feedback: confirmation echo; bad grade → structured stderr.
- version: schema (cli_version, embedding_model, latest_index_hash); missing latest → structured stderr.

Blocks on: main.py.

title: CR-013 B1: CLI --window N flag + config default + validation
description:
From CR-013 (docs/specs/change_requests/staged/cr-013_cli_retrieval_window_and_proximity_dedupe.md), Story 1 flag-surface slice.

Scope:
- Add --window N option to scripvec query (Typer).
- Read default N from project-root config file (.scripvec_config.json); no hardcoded default.
- Validate: negative N raises via cli/errors.py emit_error (ADR-001, no silent failure).
- Do NOT yet fetch adjacent verses — thread the validated integer through to the query pipeline only.

File(s):
- cli/query_cmd.py
- config loader / .scripvec_config.json default entry

Acceptance:
- Contract test in cli/test_contracts.py: --window parses; --window -1 raises and exits non-zero with ADR-001-shaped error; flag absent → effective N comes from config.
- No response-shape change yet (that lands in B3).

Depends on: (none).
Blocks: B3 (response wiring needs this flag plumbed).

title: cli/query_log.py — JSONL append for queries
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/query_log.py`.

Per CR-002. CLI-boundary concern (not in retrieval — design note in CR-001). Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class QueryLogRecord` — timestamp, schema_version="1", session_id, query_id, index_hash, mode, query, k, results (verse_id + per-component ranks + rrf_score), latency_ms.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ResultLogRow`.
- `append(record)` — atomic JSONL line write to `paths.logs_dir() / "queries.jsonl"`. Creates parents if absent. Raises on OSError (no silent swallow).
- `new_session_id()`, `new_query_id()` — UUID4 hex.

Blocks on: paths.py (only out-of-CLI dep).

title: CR-015 BEAD A: Parse and validate --hybrid-weight flag
description:
Add `--hybrid-weight <lexical:dense>` to `scripvec query`. Parser accepts either integer ratios or free-form floats (decide and document in the help text). Validation raises per ADR-001 on: (a) flag used with `--mode bm25` or `--mode dense`, (b) malformed value (non-numeric, missing half, negative, more than one colon), (c) both-zero `0:0`. No retrieval runs if validation fails.

Acceptance criteria:
- `scripvec query --mode hybrid --hybrid-weight 2:1 "..."` parses successfully; the parsed (2.0, 1.0) pair is visible to downstream fusion code (unit test confirms).
- `scripvec query --mode bm25 --hybrid-weight 1:1 "..."` exits non-zero with a message naming the mode/flag conflict.
- Malformed inputs (`foo`, `1:`, `-1:2`, `0:0`, `1:2:3`) each exit non-zero with a message that includes the rejected input.
- A chosen format (integer-ratio or free-form float) is documented in `--help` and pinned in a contract test.

Source: CR-015, Story 1.

title: retrieval/bm25.py — BM25S wrapper
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/bm25.py`.

Per CR-002. Implement:
- `@dataclass class Bm25Index` (opaque; holds BM25S retriever + index-aligned verse_id corpus mapping).
- `build_bm25(verses, index_dir)` — `k1=1.5`, `b=0.75`. Tokenizes via `tokenizer.tokenize`. Persist in BM25S native on-disk format + sidecar `verse_ids.json`. Raises on empty corpus.
- `load_bm25(index_dir) -> Bm25Index` — strict; raises on missing or version mismatch.
- `bm25_topk(idx, query, k=50) -> list[tuple[str, float]]` — tokenize query + score + map to `verse_id`. Tiebreak by `verse_id` ascending (ADR-007).

Unit-untested per CR-001 (integration surface). Blocks on: tokenizer.py, verse.py.

title: cr-014: retrieval/hybrid.py — apply exclusion pre-RRF to both BM25 and dense streams
description:
CR-014 B8. Extend the hybrid retrieval path so that when --exclude is supplied under --mode hybrid:
- The exclusion set is computed exactly as in B2 (single vector search against the exclude embedding, top exclude_m from config).
- The exclusion set is applied to the BM25 result stream AND the dense result stream BEFORE RRF fusion runs.
- RRF fuses the two already-filtered streams.
- No excluded verse can re-enter via the other stream.
- Response JSON exclude block is populated identically to B5.

Same k + exclude_buffer sizing policy as dense (B4). No hybrid-specific tuning knobs in this CR.

Blocks on: B2, B3, B4. Blocks: B12, B13.

title: cli/query_cmd.py — wire --volume flag
description:
Thread a --volume flag through the scripvec query Typer command. Build Scope(volume=...) via F1, validate via F2, pass to the retrieval path which applies F3 / F4. Pass-through unchanged when --volume is absent.

Acceptance (Story A):
- scripvec query "..." --volume book_of_mormon returns only Book of Mormon hits.
- Hits from any other volume are dropped after retrieval, before top-K.
- If fewer than K hits survive, the response returns however many survive (possibly zero); no padding.
- Unknown volume raises per ADR-001 with a specific message (message content is in F2 bead).
- Omitting --volume preserves current behavior.
- Contract test in cli/test_contracts.py covers the flag.

Source: CR-011 Story A (bead A1).

title: retrieval/build.py + test — build_index + corpus-drift guard
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/build.py` and `test_build.py`.

Per CR-002 + CR-001 § Function plan. Implement:
- `build_index(*, from_scratch=True, rebuild_corpus=False) -> str` — full sequence: corpus-drift check → iter_verses + chunker for non-verse text → assemble Manifest → config_hash → serial per-record embed (verse-chunk calls marked for ADR-015 telemetry) → transactional `insert_batch` in `corpus.sqlite` → `build_bm25` → `write_manifest` → `set_latest` only on success. Returns the new config_hash.
- `_assemble_manifest(cfg, commit_sha) -> Manifest`.
- `_drift_check_corpus(stored, observed, rebuild_corpus)` — no-op on from-scratch (no stored commit); else commits must match unless rebuild_corpus=True. Raises naming both commits.
- `from_scratch=False` raises `NotImplementedError` — incremental is out of MVP.
- **Serial embedding invariant (ADR-006)**: plain Python loop; no ThreadPoolExecutor/asyncio.gather. Verified by review.
- **Atomic publish**: `set_latest` only on the final success path.

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded: drift-guard passes on match / no stored / rebuild_corpus=True; raises on mismatch with both commits in message.

Blocks on: embed, embed_telemetry, manifest, store, bm25, bcbooks, chunker.

title: cli/main.py — Typer root + subcommand registration
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/main.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADR-007. Construct `app = typer.Typer(add_completion=False, pretty_exceptions_enable=False, no_args_is_help=True)`. At import, call `register(app)` on each subcommand module (`query_cmd`, `index_cmd`, `eval_cmd`, `feedback_cmd`, `version_cmd`).

Wired to console script `scripvec = "scripvec_cli.main:app"` (already in pyproject.toml). No wrapper function, no __main__.py.

Blocks on: all subcommand modules.

title: cli/feedback_cmd.py — scripvec feedback
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/feedback_cmd.py`.

Per CR-002. Flags `--query-id, --verse-id, --grade {0,1,2}, --note`. Appends one record to `data/logs/feedback.jsonl` (timestamp, schema_version, query_id, verse_id, grade, note), prints JSON confirmation. Bad grade → USER_ERROR via emit_error.

Blocks on: paths.py, errors.py.

title: eval/metrics.py + test — recall@k, nDCG@10, MRR@10, percentile
description:
Files: `packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/metrics.py` and `test_metrics.py`.

Per CR-002 (0/1/2 graded; absence = 0). Pure functions:
- `recall_at_k(retrieved, relevant, k)`.
- `ndcg_at_10(retrieved, relevant)` — `gain(grade) = 2**grade - 1`.
- `mrr_at_10(retrieved, relevant)`.
- `percentile(samples, p)`.

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded test coverage: known-fixture values, empty-relevant returns 0.0, perfect-order nDCG = 1.0, gain formula (grade-2 → 3, grade-1 → 1), MRR inverse rank, percentile on known samples, ValueErrors on bad args.

title: retrieval/embed_telemetry.py — ADR-015 running average
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/embed_telemetry.py`.

Per ADR-015. The embed client's per-verse-chunk latency instrumentation. Implement:
- Record per-call timing (request initiation → normalized vector returned).
- Maintain a running average scoped per active `(endpoint, model, dim, normalize)` config tuple.
- Append-only per-call timing log under `paths.logs_dir()`; separate running-average state file.
- Config change resets the live average; prior-config average retained for audit.
- Startup re-derive path from the append-only log when the running-average file is missing; raise on corruption (ADR-001).
- **Exception carve-out per ADR-015 §5**: telemetry-write failure logs at warn and does NOT raise (embed call itself must not be penalized). Startup corruption DOES raise.

Blocks on: paths.py. CLI surface to read the live average is wired later via `scripvec stats embed` or equivalent; this bead only produces the storage + update API the embed client calls.

title: cr-014: config — add exclude_m and exclude_buffer keys to project-root config schema
description:
CR-014 B1. Add two new keys to the scripvec project-root config file schema:
- exclude_m: int — top-M verses used as the exclusion set when --exclude is supplied. No numeric target in CR; pick a sane starting default, tunable.
- exclude_buffer: int — extra hits the main query internally retrieves before exclusion so the final top-K is not starved.

Per project memory: ADRs lock policies, not numeric values. These keys live in the project-root config, not in an ADR.

Acceptance:
- Config loader accepts both keys and raises on malformed values (ADR-001 loud failure).
- Missing key falls back to a default defined in exactly one place in code.
- Unit test: round-trip load with keys returns expected values; malformed value raises.

Covers prereq for S1 (dense) and S3 (hybrid). Blocks: B2, B4.

title: CR-015 BEAD B: Apply weighted RRF in hybrid fusion
description:
Multiply each retriever's RRF rank contribution by its weight before summing. Default weight (when the flag is absent) comes from the project-root config file — no new config key introduced here; reuse whatever key holds the current default balance.

Acceptance criteria:
- `--hybrid-weight 1:1` produces output byte-identical to the prior default-weighted RRF on a canned query set (regression test).
- `--hybrid-weight 10:1` on a lexical-heavy canned query produces a ranking closer to BM25-only than the `1:1` case does (rank-distance metric, checked against a fixture).
- `--hybrid-weight 1:10` on a semantic canned query produces a ranking closer to dense-only than `1:1` does.
- `--mode bm25` and `--mode dense` outputs are byte-identical to prior outputs for the same inputs (no accidental leak of the weight into non-hybrid paths).

Source: CR-015, Story 2.

title: retrieval/query.py + test — query path, drift guard, ADR-013/014
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/query.py` and `test_query.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADRs 013/014 + CR-001 § Function plan. Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class QueryResult` with CR-002 output shape (query, mode, k, index, results tuple, latency_ms).
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ResultRow` — rank, verse_id, ref, text, score, scores dict; **plus `forced: bool` marker per ADR-014**.
- `query(text, *, k=10, mode="hybrid", index="latest") -> QueryResult`:
  1. `_resolve_index` → (hash, dir).
  2. Load manifest; `_drift_check_endpoint` vs runtime EmbedConfig (ADR-001). Raise on mismatch.
  3. **ADR-013 extraction**: `reference.extract_references(text)` → resolved verse set. Grammatical-but-nonexistent raises.
  4. Dispatch on mode: `_run_bm25`, `_run_dense` (via embed + dense_topk), rrf. Capture per-stage latency.
  5. **ADR-014 force-inclusion**: merge extracted verse set into results; dedupe; mark each row `forced: True|False` (True if came from extraction — both if dedupe collision); default position = top in query-appearance order; default count contract = additive (may return `k + N_extracted`).
  6. Return CR-002 shape; `latency_ms` has bm25/dense/fuse/total keys (0.0 for absent stages — stable schema).
- Private `_resolve_index`, `_drift_check_endpoint`, `_run_bm25`, `_run_dense`.

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded: drift-check on url/model/dim mismatch (names field + both values); resolve latest/explicit-hash; missing directory raises. **Also ADR-014 tests**: force-included also-organically-retrieved dedupes to one row with both markers; force-included-not-organic adds an extra row; no extraction → organic unchanged; range overlap partial.

Blocks on: config, paths, embed, manifest, store, bm25, rrf, reference (extract).

title: Workspace wiring: add runtime deps and sync
description:
Serial; all Sprint 1 work depends on this. Add runtime deps to package pyprojects per CR-001 System 1:
- `packages/retrieval/pyproject.toml`: `httpx`, `sqlite-vec`, `bm25s`, `numpy`.
- `apps/scripvec_cli/pyproject.toml`: `typer`.
- Other packages: verify no deps needed at runtime.

Run `uv sync` at workspace root; confirm a single lockfile resolves. Confirm `data/indexes/`, `data/logs/` gitignored; `data/raw/bcbooks/*.json` and `data/eval/*.jsonl` tracked (ADR-003).

Accept: `uv sync` green; `uv run python -c 'import typer, httpx, sqlite_vec, bm25s, numpy'` succeeds from workspace root.

title: CLI contract tests pinning ADR-001 and ADR-007 posture for --floor
description:
Slice: contract tests that fail loudly if --floor drifts from spec.

Depends on: sv-lo9, sv-t2l, sv-amu, sv-l73, sv-kd7.

Acceptance criteria:
- Test that every out-of-range / malformed --floor value exits non-zero with the expected message (ADR-001).
- Test that the response always carries the 'floor' key (null or populated) and matches the snapshot shape (ADR-007).
- Tests are wired into the same cli/test_contracts.py harness as existing CLI contract tests.
- Failure output on mismatch names the drifted field, not just 'assertion failed'.

Source: docs/specs/change_requests/staged/cr-012_cli_mode_aware_similarity_floor.md (Bead B6).

title: CR-013 B8: docs — README guidance for scripvec query
description:
From CR-013 Story 7 (documentation).

Scope:
- Update the scripvec query README section (project README.md or query-specific readme — engineer picks based on repo convention).
- Cover THREE points in prose:
  1. Dedupe runs BEFORE window, and why: no work wasted on windows for hits that dedupe would drop; predictable payload shape.
  2. Study-flow recommendation: when both --window and --dedupe are on, --k 1..3 gives richer context than --k 10 with narrow windows. Frame as a recommendation, NOT an enforced rule. The CLI must not cap or warn on higher --k.
  3. Default-on nature of --dedupe, and --no-dedupe as the escape hatch for callers who want raw retrieval order.

File(s):
- README.md (or query-specific docs)

Acceptance:
- All three points present and accurate.
- No code changes.

Depends on: B1 (sv-b4q), B5 (sv-pc6) — flags must exist in code before docs describe them.
Blocks: (none). Can be authored in parallel with B7.

title: cli/errors.py — ExitCode + emit_error
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/errors.py`.

Per ADR-007 + CR-002. Implement:
- `class ExitCode` with `SUCCESS=0, USER_ERROR=1, NOT_FOUND=2, UPSTREAM_ERROR=3`.
- `emit_error(code, message, details=None, exit_code=USER_ERROR) -> NoReturn` — writes `{"error":{"code","message","details"}}` JSON to stderr, calls `sys.exit`.

No in-repo imports. Single writer so error shape can't drift.

title: data/eval/judgments.jsonl — graded relevance
description:
File: `data/eval/judgments.jsonl` (authored content).

Per CR-002. One JSON object per line: `{query_id, verse_id, grade}` with `grade ∈ {1, 2}` (`0` = absent from file, per ADR).

- Grade 1 = relevant; 2 = canonical/definitive.
- Every `verse_id` must exist in the live corpus.
- Every `query_id` must exist in `queries.jsonl`.

Blocks on: queries.jsonl (needs query_ids) + a built index (needs verse_ids).

title: Response shape: populate floor field with value, interpretation, effective_threshold
description:
Slice: JSON response schema change. Adds 'floor' as a top-level key on every response.

Depends on: sv-lo9 (flag surface). Should land alongside or immediately after the first mode-specific bead (sv-t2l / dense is simplest).

Acceptance criteria:
- Every response carries top-level 'floor' key.
- When --floor is absent, 'floor' is null.
- When --floor is set, 'floor' is an object with value, interpretation, effective_threshold.
- 'interpretation' is exactly 'absolute' under dense, exactly 'relative' under bm25 and hybrid.
- 'effective_threshold' is the concrete cutoff score in the mode's native units (cosine for dense; BM25 score for bm25; RRF score for hybrid).
- When --floor is set but 'results' is empty (all-culled or zero-hit), the 'floor' field is still populated with the same shape.
- Snapshot fixtures under: no-floor, floor-keeps-all, floor-culls-some, floor-culls-all — one per mode once sv-t2l / sv-amu / sv-l73 land.

Rationale: separating the response-shape bead from the semantic beads lets the JSON contract be reviewed independently of the cutoff logic.

Open question flagged from CR: the interpretation label may change to 'cosine'/'ratio' during implementation. If so, update acceptance criteria here; don't paper over with a mapping layer.

Source: docs/specs/change_requests/staged/cr-012_cli_mode_aware_similarity_floor.md (Bead B5).

title: cr-014: cli/query.py — wire --exclude flag through dense-mode path
description:
CR-014 B4. Add --exclude <text> to the scripvec query CLI and wire it through the dense path.

Behavior under --mode dense with --exclude present:
- Call compute_exclusion_set(text, exclude_m_from_config) via the retrieval layer (B2).
- The main dense query internally retrieves k + exclude_buffer hits (exclude_buffer from B1).
- Apply filter_by_exclusion (B3) to the dense result list.
- Take top-K from what survives.

Behavior under --mode dense without --exclude: unchanged.

Absent --exclude: no exclude block in response (see B5).

ADR-006: the main query embed and the exclude embed run sequentially. This bead chooses the serialization point (recommend: exclude-embed first, then main-embed, so the exclusion set is already computed when main hits return — but order is not locked). B13 verifies non-overlap.

Blocks on: B1, B2, B3. Blocks: B5, B8, B9, B13.

title: cr-014: cli/test_exclude_contracts.py — input validation (empty, whitespace, oversize)
description:
CR-014 B11. Contract tests for the validation paths in B7.

Cases:
- --exclude '' → user-error exit, no embed call.
- --exclude '   ' (whitespace-only) → user-error exit, no embed call.
- --exclude with text exceeding the ADR-005 8K-token cap → user-error exit, no embed call.

In all three: exit code is the user-input-error code, not the upstream-error code 3.

Blocks on: B7.

title: Hybrid-mode relative floor culls results below ratio of top RRF score
description:
Slice: applies --floor under --mode hybrid, operating on the final RRF score.

Depends on: sv-lo9 (flag surface). Independent of dense and bm25 semantic beads.

Acceptance criteria:
- Under --mode hybrid, hits with RRF score < floor.value * top_hit_rrf_score are dropped before top-K is returned.
- The cutoff is applied to the final ordering score currently used for hybrid (RRF today), not to any component score.
- --floor 0.0 is a no-op.
- Unit test: fixture hybrid query with top RRF 0.0312, --floor 0.5 drops all hits below 0.0156.

Future note: if a reranker (cr-003) lands, 'final ordering score' will mean the reranked score; a new bead replaces this one's definition at that time. Not a dependency here.

Source: docs/specs/change_requests/staged/cr-012_cli_mode_aware_similarity_floor.md (Bead B4).

title: Add --floor flag surface and validation to scripvec query
description:
Slice: CLI flag parsing plus input validation only. No semantic effect on results yet.

Acceptance criteria:
- scripvec query --floor <value> accepts a numeric value.
- Values outside [0.0, 1.0] raise with a message naming the current --mode and the accepted range [0.0, 1.0].
- Non-numeric values raise a parse error naming the flag.
- --floor 0.0 and --floor 0 parse successfully and are stored as the same internal value.
- Absence of --floor leaves the internal floor config as 'not set' (distinct from 0.0, since the response shape differs).
- A contract test covers each of: valid in-range, boundary 0.0, boundary 1.0, out-of-range high, out-of-range low, non-numeric, absent.

Rationale: landing the flag surface first lets every downstream bead (dense/bm25/hybrid semantic) be a thin semantic patch. Also satisfies Story 4's ADR-001 posture independently of which mode is wired first.

Source: docs/specs/change_requests/staged/cr-012_cli_mode_aware_similarity_floor.md (Bead B1 in the Beads plan section).

title: eval/dataset.py SANITY_PROBES values — fill in constant
description:
Edit: `packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/dataset.py` — fill in the `SANITY_PROBES` module constant with three `SanityProbe(query=..., expected_top3=(...))` tuples per CR-002. Each probe's expected verse(s) must score in the BM25 top-3.

These are a contract the harness enforces, not data a human edits later (per CR-001 § design note).

Blocks on: queries.jsonl + a built index (need real verse_ids from the live corpus).

title: CR-013 B3: response — attach window payload to each hit when N>0
description:
From CR-013 Story 1 + Story 6 (response-shape).

Scope:
- In the query response assembler, for each hit, when the effective N > 0, attach a window field with shape:
    window: {before: [{ref, text}, ...], after: [{ref, text}, ...]}
- When N == 0 or --window flag is absent → window field is OMITTED (not null, not {}).
- Text-only inside window (no per-verse score) — CR Open-Questions resolution toward text-only.
- before / after lists are in ascending scripture order, not hit-centric order.

File(s):
- query response assembler (packages/retrieval/... or cli/query_cmd.py, engineer decides based on layering)

Acceptance (contract test):
- With --window N>0: each hit has window with both keys; elements have exactly {ref, text}.
- With --window 0 (or no flag): no hit has a window field at all.
- Near-boundary hit renders a shorter list, not padded/null.

Depends on: B1 (sv-b4q — flag must be plumbed), B2 (sv-yjl — lookup helper exists).
Blocks: B7.

title: CR-015 BEAD G: Document both flags in CLI help and add examples
description:
Update `scripvec query --help` and any agent-facing docs to list `--hybrid-weight` and `--cross-ref-expand` with one-line descriptions, value-shape examples, mode/metadata dependencies, and the exact error messages users will see when they misuse the flags.

Acceptance criteria:
- `scripvec query --help` output shows both flags with examples and dependency notes.
- `--hybrid-weight` help names the `--mode hybrid` requirement.
- `--cross-ref-expand` help names the index-metadata requirement and quotes the exact failure message from BEAD E.
- Contract test pins the help-text substrings so they can't drift silently.

Source: CR-015, Story 7.

title: CR-015 BEAD D: Parse and validate --cross-ref-expand N
description:
Add `--cross-ref-expand N` to `scripvec query`. `N=0` and absence of the flag are equivalent no-ops. Validation raises on negative `N` or non-integer `N`. Accepted regardless of `--mode` (bm25, dense, hybrid).

Acceptance criteria:
- `--cross-ref-expand 0` and flag-absent both produce identical output (no `cross_references` field added; test asserts JSON equality).
- `--cross-ref-expand -1`, `--cross-ref-expand abc` each exit non-zero with a message including the rejected input.
- `--cross-ref-expand 3` is accepted under all three modes (bm25, dense, hybrid) at the parse layer.
- No retrieval runs if validation fails.

Source: CR-015, Story 4.

title: retrieval/rrf.py + test — Reciprocal Rank Fusion
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/rrf.py` and `test_rrf.py`.

Per CR-002. Pure math:
- `rrf(bm25_hits, dense_hits, *, k=60, top_k=10) -> list[tuple[str, float]]` — `score(d) = Σ 1/(k + rank_i(d))`, ties broken by `verse_id` ascending (ADR-007 deterministic). Raises `ValueError` on `k<1` or `top_k<1`.

Tests: formula on known inputs, tiebreak, top_k truncation, disjoint inputs, ValueError on bad args.

title: retrieval/__init__.py — public re-exports
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/__init__.py`.

Per CR-001 § Module-boundary contracts. Re-export: `build_index`, `query`, `QueryResult`, `ResultRow`. **Do NOT re-export `embed`** (ADR-006, CR-001 § design question 5 resolved hidden).

Blocks on: build.py, query.py.

title: cli/test_contracts.py — --volume / --book / --range composition tests
description:
Black-box contract tests for every pairwise and triple combination of the scope flags, asserting intersection semantics.

Acceptance (Story D):
- volume+book: hits are in both.
- volume+range: hits are in both.
- book+range: hits are in both.
- volume+book+range: hits are in the intersection of all three.
- Internal retrieval buffer is large enough that top-K fills when the scoped slice supports it (F4 contract).
- Each test also verifies the scope field in the response (coordinate with E1 if E1 merges first).

Source: CR-011 Story D (bead D1).

title: cr-014: cli/query.py — validate --exclude input (empty, whitespace, oversize)
description:
CR-014 B7. Validate --exclude at the CLI boundary before any embed call is made.

Cases:
- Empty string (--exclude '') → raise with a message naming the flag and the empty-string problem. User-error exit code.
- Whitespace-only string → treat as empty; raise same.
- Text exceeding the ADR-005 8K-token cap → raise per ADR-005, reusing the same measurement used by the main query dense path (do not invent a new tokenizer here).

In all three cases: no embed call is made; exit code is the user-input-error code (not upstream-error code 3).

Blocks on: none from this CR. Blocks: B11.

title: CR-013 B5: CLI --dedupe / --no-dedupe flags, default on, mutually exclusive
description:
From CR-013 Story 3 (flag surface).

Scope:
- Add --dedupe / --no-dedupe to scripvec query (Typer).
- Default: dedupe ON (so bare scripvec query ... dedupes by default).
- Mutually exclusive: passing both raises and exits non-zero with an ADR-001-shaped error (no silent failure).
- Does NOT yet wire to dedupe machinery — surface the boolean to the pipeline only.

File(s):
- cli/query_cmd.py

Acceptance (contract test in cli/test_contracts.py):
- No flag → effective dedupe = True.
- --dedupe → True.
- --no-dedupe → False.
- --dedupe --no-dedupe → error, exit non-zero.

Depends on: (none).
Blocks: B6.

title: cr-014: cli/test_exclude_contracts.py — BM25 rejection
description:
CR-014 B10. Contract test: scripvec query '<main>' --exclude '<avoid>' --mode bm25 exits with the user-error code and emits the error message specified in B6. No embed call; no vector search.

Blocks on: B6.

title: Docs: --floor flag reference and mode-aware semantics
description:
Slice: user-facing documentation entry for --floor.

Depends on: sv-t2l, sv-amu, sv-l73 (to describe real behavior, not aspirational).

Acceptance criteria:
- --floor is listed in the CLI help text for 'scripvec query' with a one-line summary that mentions mode-dependence.
- Long-form docs (wherever CLI flags are documented in this repo) describe:
  - dense mode: absolute cosine; [0.0, 1.0]
  - bm25 mode: relative to top hit
  - hybrid mode: relative to top hit's RRF score
- The response's 'floor' field is documented with an example JSON block.
- Out-of-range values are documented as errors, not warnings.

Source: docs/specs/change_requests/staged/cr-012_cli_mode_aware_similarity_floor.md (Bead B7).

title: CR-015 BEAD C: Echo applied hybrid_weight in JSON response
description:
Add `hybrid_weight` to the top-level response for `--mode hybrid`. When the flag is supplied, the field carries `{"lexical": N, "dense": M}`. When the flag is absent, the field carries a chosen sentinel (either `null` or `{"lexical": D, "dense": E, "from_config": true}`) — pick one at implementation time and pin. Field is absent on `--mode bm25` / `--mode dense`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Contract test under `cli/test_contracts.py` covers (a) flag supplied, (b) flag absent with `--mode hybrid`, (c) non-hybrid mode (field absent).
- Help text documents the echo field.
- The sentinel-form choice is documented in the CR audit log once pinned.

Source: CR-015, Story 3.

title: retrieval/scope_filter.py — post-retrieval scope filter
description:
Given a list of candidate hits (verse rows with volume / book / chapter / verse fields) and a canonical Scope, drop hits outside the scope. Preserve order within kept hits.

Acceptance:
- Volume-only, book-only, range-only scopes each filter correctly.
- All three pairwise compositions (volume+book, volume+range, book+range) filter correctly.
- Triple composition filters correctly (intersection of all three).
- Edge cases: inclusive boundaries at chapter start / end; verse-specified range endpoints are verse-inclusive per ADR-011.
- Hits order within the kept set is preserved.

Source: CR-011 Beads plan foundation F3.

title: retrieval/store.py — sqlite-vec + verses table
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/store.py`.

Per CR-002 persistence. Implement:
- `@dataclass class StoreConn` holding the sqlite3 connection with `sqlite-vec` extension loaded.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class DenseHit` — verse_id/rowid/cosine/record.
- `open_store(path, *, create=False) -> StoreConn` — opens or creates `corpus.sqlite` with `verses(rowid, verse_id, ref_canonical, book, chapter, verse, text)` and `vec0 float[dim]` virtual table. Dim read from EmbedConfig, not hardcoded. Raises if extension load fails or on dim drift.
- `insert_batch(conn, rows)` — transactional; verses row + vec0 row in same txn per batch, keyed by same rowid. Fails loud on dim mismatch (ADR-001).
- `dense_topk(conn, query_vec, k=50) -> list[DenseHit]` — inner-product top-k joined to verses in one SQL. Inner product over L2-normalized vecs is cosine (ADR-005).
- `get_verse(conn, verse_id) -> VerseRecord` — raises on missing row.

Unit-untested per CR-001 (integration surface). Blocks on: config.py, verse.py.

title: cr-014: retrieval/test_hybrid_exclude.py — exclusion applied pre-RRF
description:
CR-014 B12. Contract test for the hybrid path under --mode hybrid with --exclude (B8).

Assertions:
- No excluded verse appears in the final top-K, regardless of whether it would have come from BM25, dense, or both streams.
- Exclusion is applied to each stream BEFORE RRF fusion — verify by mocking or instrumenting the fusion step so the test can observe the pre-fusion inputs.
- Response JSON exclude block is identical in shape to dense mode.

Blocks on: B8.

title: cli/query_cmd.py — wire --book flag
description:
Thread --book through the scripvec query command. Reuse F1/F2/F3/F4. Book scope applies within whatever volume scope is in effect, or all volumes if the book name is unique across volumes and no --volume is supplied.

Acceptance (Story B):
- scripvec query "..." --book alma returns only Alma hits.
- --book against D&C raises with exact message: "D&C has sections, not books; use --range instead".
- Unknown book raises per ADR-001 (fuzz interaction per cr-008 if that ships).
- --book against a volume the book does not belong to raises per ADR-001.
- Contract test covers the flag.

Source: CR-011 Story B (bead B1). Shares the CLI file with A1 which establishes the wiring pattern.

title: Dense-mode absolute floor culls results below cosine threshold
description:
Slice: applies --floor under --mode dense only. BM25 and hybrid still ignore the value (tracked in sibling beads).

Depends on: sv-lo9 (flag surface and validation).

Acceptance criteria:
- Under --mode dense, hits with cosine score < floor.value are dropped before top-K is returned.
- --floor 0.0 is a no-op; behavior is identical to absence of the flag except that the response 'floor' field is populated with value 0.0.
- When all hits are culled, 'results' is an empty list; no error.
- Unit test: fixture query returning hits at [0.82, 0.71, 0.55, 0.42, 0.31], --floor 0.55 keeps three hits.
- Unit test: all-culled case returns empty 'results' with 'floor' populated.

Source: docs/specs/change_requests/staged/cr-012_cli_mode_aware_similarity_floor.md (Bead B2).

title: cli/index_cmd.py — scripvec index build + list
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/index_cmd.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADR-007. Two subcommands under one `index` Typer sub-app:
- `index build` flags `--from-scratch`, `--rebuild-corpus`. Calls `retrieval.build_index`. Prints `{index_hash, latest}` JSON on success.
- `index list` — walks `paths.indexes_dir()/*/config.json`, reads each manifest, prints JSON array of `{hash, created_at, model, dim, is_latest}` sorted by hash asc. `created_at` from file mtime per CR-001 § design question 2.

Error map: drift RuntimeError → USER_ERROR `corpus_drift`; embed → UPSTREAM_ERROR `embedding_endpoint`; else USER_ERROR `build_failed`.

Blocks on: errors, retrieval.build, retrieval.paths, retrieval.manifest.

title: Build and validate — run ship criteria
description:
Per CR-001 § Implementation plan step 10. Final validation bead.

Steps:
1. `scripvec index build --from-scratch` — confirm `data/indexes/<hash>/` contains `corpus.sqlite`, `bm25.bm25s`, `config.json`; confirm `data/indexes/latest` points at it.
2. Smoke: `scripvec query "faith and works" --k 10` — confirm JSON validates against documented schema.
3. `scripvec eval run --queries data/eval/queries.jsonl --judgments data/eval/judgments.jsonl`.
4. Report each ship criterion in eval output:
   a. hybrid beats BM25 by ≥5 pts recall@10.
   b. dense beats BM25 by ≥5 pts recall@10.
   c. index dir ≤400 MB on disk.

Do NOT paper over failures (ADR-001). A failing criterion is the signal — record it in the CR-001 audit log and open follow-up CRs per the project's workflow. Per ADR-005 "response to poor dense performance is not to swap models on the current endpoint" — either switch endpoints or invest in non-model quality axes.

Blocks on: every earlier bead.

title: cli/query_cmd.py — echo scope in response JSON
description:
Add a 'scope' object to the scripvec query response JSON. Each of scope.volume, scope.book, scope.range is the canonical normalized form of what was passed, or null if that flag was omitted.

Acceptance (Story E):
- scope is always present in the query response object (even on unscoped queries, where all three fields are null).
- Canonicalization comes from F1.
- The field is additive per ADR-007: no breaking change to existing consumers.
- Example from the CR roundtrips: {"scope": {"volume": "book_of_mormon", "book": "alma", "range": "30-42"}}.

Source: CR-011 Story E (bead E1).

title: CR-015 BEAD E: Loud-fail when index lacks cross-reference metadata
description:
Detect at the index level (capability flag / schema marker) whether cross-reference metadata is present. If `--cross-ref-expand N > 0` is used against an index that lacks it, raise with the exact message:

`cross-reference metadata not present in this index — rebuild with cross-reference ingestion enabled`

Error surfaces on stderr (or the agent-facing error channel per ADR-007), not in a successful JSON body. Exit is non-zero.

Acceptance criteria:
- Contract test simulates an index-without-metadata and asserts the exact error string, non-zero exit.
- Detection is O(1) at index open — no per-verse scan.
- When an index *does* carry metadata, this check is a no-op and does not block retrieval.

Source: CR-015, Story 5.

title: cli/query_cmd.py — scripvec query
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/query_cmd.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADR-007. Flags: positional text, `--k 10`, `--mode {bm25|dense|hybrid}` default hybrid, `--format {json|text}` default json, `--index {<hash>|latest}` default latest, `--show-scores`.

Implement `register(app)` + private `_run_query`, `_to_log_record`, `_format_text`. Call `retrieval.query`, format to CR-002 JSON on stdout, append one record to `queries.jsonl`. ADR-007 help text: purpose/flags/schema/exit codes/JSON example.

Error map: ValueError flags → USER_ERROR `bad_flag`; missing index → NOT_FOUND `index_not_found`; drift/embed → UPSTREAM_ERROR `endpoint_drift` or `embedding_endpoint`.

Blocks on: errors, query_log, retrieval.query.

title: retrieval/hashing.py + test — canonical_json + blake2b_128_hex
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/hashing.py` and `test_hashing.py`.

Per CR-001 § File blueprint (Ousterhout split for testability):
- `canonical_json(obj) -> bytes` — sorted keys, no whitespace, UTF-8.
- `blake2b_128_hex(payload: bytes) -> str` — 32-char lowercase hex digest.

Test file per CR-001 § Expanded test coverage: sort-keys invariance, no-whitespace, UTF-8 round trip, 32-char length, determinism, composition invariance.

No in-repo imports. Pure functions.

title: cr-014: cli/query.py — add exclude block to JSON response (dense mode)
description:
CR-014 B5. Extend the scripvec query JSON response to include an 'exclude' block when --exclude is supplied.

Shape:
{
  "exclude": {
    "text": "<verbatim echo of --exclude input>",
    "set_size": <applied exclude_m>,
    "excluded_verse_ids": ["<verse_id>", ...]
  },
  ...existing fields unchanged...
}

Rules:
- Block is absent (key not present, not null) when --exclude is not supplied.
- set_size equals the applied exclude_m from config.
- excluded_verse_ids lists exactly the verse IDs that compute_exclusion_set returned, so the caller can audit the filter.
- Empty-results case (B9 covers test): results: [] with exclude block still fully populated is valid, not an error.

ADR-007: one additive block; no breaking change to any existing field.

Blocks on: B4. Blocks: B9.

title: retrieval/tokenizer.py + test — BM25 tokenization
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/tokenizer.py` and `test_tokenizer.py`.

Per CR-002 BM25 rules. Implement:
- `tokenize(text: str) -> list[str]` — pure function: lowercase → Unicode NFC → split on `\W+` → drop empties → drop pure-digit tokens < 2 chars. Stopwords off.

Tests per CR-001: lowercasing, NFC normalization, word-boundary split, drop short digit tokens, keep alphanumerics, determinism.

title: CR-013 B6: wire dedupe + emit top-level dedupe envelope field
description:
From CR-013 Story 2 + Story 3 + Story 5 (dedupe wired + response envelope).

Scope:
- Wire B4's dedupe function into the query pipeline behind B5's boolean.
- When dedupe is ON: pull k_buffer candidates, dedupe, cut to k.
- When dedupe is OFF: no drops; top-K taken from raw sorted retrieval.
- Add top-level response field:
    dedupe: {enabled: bool, proximity_verses: int, dropped: int}
  on EVERY query response — regardless of --window or --dedupe state.
- When --no-dedupe: enabled=false, dropped=0, proximity_verses echoes the configured M (so callers can still read the config value).

File(s):
- query orchestrator (packages/retrieval/... query entrypoint)
- response assembler

Acceptance (integration test):
- Dedupe-on path: dropped > 0 when candidates contain a near-cluster; kept hits are the higher-scored ones.
- Dedupe-off path: dropped == 0; kept hits match raw score-desc order.
- envelope field present and well-shaped in both cases.

Depends on: B4 (sv-1v1), B5 (sv-pc6).
Blocks: B7, B9.

title: corpus_ingest/bcbooks.py — JSON walker + commit SHA
description:
File: `packages/corpus_ingest/src/scripvec_corpus_ingest/bcbooks.py`.

Per CR-001 § Function plan. Walks `data/raw/bcbooks/{book-of-mormon,doctrine-and-covenants}.json`:
- `iter_verses(data_dir) -> Iterator[VerseRecord]` — yields BoM then D&C, canonical-book order, chapter/verse ascending. Invariants per `VerseRecord`.
- `corpus_commit_sha(data_dir) -> str` — git SHA of `data/raw/bcbooks/`. Raises if not a git repo, no commit touches path (ADR-001).
- Private `_iter_book_of_mormon`, `_iter_doctrine_and_covenants`.

Raises: FileNotFoundError on missing JSON; ValueError on JSON shape mismatch.

Blocks on: verse.py, reference.py (canonicalize_book + canonical).

title: reference/books.py — canonical book table
description:
File: `packages/reference/src/scripvec_reference/books.py`.

Per CR-001 § File blueprint, ADR-010 strict canonical parser. Implement:
- `CANONICAL_BOOKS: tuple[str, ...]` — BoM books in canonical order (1 Nephi → Moroni), then `D&C`.
- `canonicalize_book(raw: str) -> str` — maps every spelling present in `data/raw/bcbooks/*.json` to the pinned canonical form. Case-sensitive per ADR-010. Raises `ValueError` on unknown input (ADR-001).
- Private `_NAME_TABLE: dict[str, str]` lookup.

Accept: unit covering positive (every canonical book), negative (abbrev, case variants per ADR-010's rejection list).

title: CR-013 B2: retrieval/window.py — verse-neighbor lookup helper
description:
From CR-013 Story 1 (data-access slice).

Scope:
- New module: packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/window.py
- Pure function: given verse_id and N, return up to N verses before and N verses after, bounded by chapter/section. Does not cross chapter boundaries.
- Reads from the verses table in corpus.sqlite.
- Returns lists of {ref, text} in scripture order. Text-only — no per-verse score included.

Acceptance (unit tests in same package):
- verse at position 1 of chapter → before is [], after has N entries (or fewer if chapter shorter than N+1).
- verse at last position of chapter → after is [], before has N entries (or fewer).
- N larger than chapter length → clip cleanly, no error.
- N == 0 → both lists empty.
- Two hits that are neighbors: independent calls return independent window payloads (no stitching).

Depends on: (none — can be authored in parallel with B1).
Blocks: B3.

title: data/eval/queries.jsonl — authored 50-query floor
description:
File: `data/eval/queries.jsonl` (authored content, not a module).

Per CR-002 § Evaluation harness + CR-001 System 9. Author 50-query floor (75 target). One JSON object per line: `{query_id, query, tags, notes?}`.

Coverage requirements (enforced by `load_queries`):
- ≥8 queries in each of four canonical buckets: `doctrinal`, `narrative`, `phrase-memory`, `proper-noun`.
- No duplicate `query` strings.
- No duplicate `query_id`s.

Author queries that exercise the named failure modes (CR-002): proper-noun retrieval (Teancum, Coriantumr), phrase-memory ("arise and shine forth"), doctrinal themes, narrative recall.

Blocks on: nothing code-side. Can start once eval.dataset defines the JSONL schema (bead 020).

title: retrieval/embed.py + test — sanctioned embed client
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/embed.py` and `test_embed.py`.

Per ADRs 005, 006, 001, 015. The single sanctioned entry point to the embedding endpoint. Implement:
- `embed(text: str) -> list[float]` — synchronous, serial, L2-normalized, dim-checked. No async, no batch, no retry.
- `_estimate_token_count(text) -> int` — conservative upper bound (char/3 + word_count).
- `_post_embedding(cfg, text) -> list[float]` — raw POST + parse of `data[0].embedding`.
- `_l2_normalize(vec) -> list[float]` — raises on zero norm.
- **ADR-015 hook**: on successful verse-chunk embed call, update running average via `embed_telemetry` module. Use a marker arg or second public entry point to distinguish verse-chunk calls from query/non-verse calls.

Raises (ADR-005/001): `RuntimeError` on >8K token input, non-2xx HTTP, dim mismatch, malformed response.

Tests (ADR-005 validation): wrong-length response raises with observed+expected dims, oversized input raises without HTTP call, non-2xx raises with status in message, normalized vector returned on success.

Blocks on: config.py.

title: retrieval/scope.py — scope-consistency validation (ADR-001 messages)
description:
Validate that the three scope fields do not contradict each other. Raise with the specific messages required by Story G per ADR-001.

Required messages:
- '"D&C has sections, not books; use --range instead"' when --book is supplied against D&C (exact string).
- Book-not-in-volume: message names both the book and the conflicting volume.
- Range-outside-volume-or-book: message names the conflicting filter and the reference.
- Unknown volume, unknown book, malformed range: distinct messages each.

Acceptance:
- Every Story G failure case has a unit test asserting the exact message substring.
- No scope-consistency failure is silent; every one raises.

Source: CR-011 Beads plan foundation F2.
